Brand Style Kit
Version 2.0 | 2026
01. Logo
The Third Day
Three ascending arcs represent the three days, each growing stronger. A bold cross breaks through the final arc, rising above. Three small dots at the crown represent the Trinity: Father, Son, and Holy Spirit.
Primary, Light Background
Primary, Dark Background
Icon Mark, Clay
RISEN
CHURCH
Horizontal Lockup
Clear Space

Minimum Clear Space

Always maintain a clear zone around the logo equal to the height of the cross arm. This ensures the mark remains legible and uncluttered in all applications. Minimum size: 32px width for digital, 12mm for print.

02. Color Palette
Earthy Nature
These colors are meant to feel grounded and natural, like walking the same dusty roads and sitting under the same open sky. The palette puts you in the moment, close to the earth and close to Him.

Option A is the original warm earth tone palette. Option B introduces a cooler, twilight-inspired direction that feels contemplative and modern.

03. Typography
Voice & Tone
Three font pairing options for the team to compare. Each brings a different personality, from refined elegance to warm approachability to editorial gravitas.
Display: Cormorant Garamond
Come as you are.
A refined, elegant serif with high contrast. Brings timeless spiritual gravitas. Use for headlines, hero text, sermon titles, and key messaging.

Aa

Body: Outfit
Welcome home.
Clean geometric sans-serif. Approachable and contemporary while remaining highly legible at small sizes. Use for body copy, navigation, buttons, and UI text.

Come as you are.
Bold and grounded with a classic feel. DM Serif Display is warmer and more approachable than Cormorant. It feels less editorial and more personal. Great for a church that wants to feel established but welcoming.

Display: Fraunces
Come as you are.
A soft serif with wonky, organic character. Fraunces has a handcrafted, slightly playful quality that feels distinctly human. It avoids the formal rigidity of traditional serifs while still carrying weight and meaning. Excellent for a church community that values authenticity.

Body: Nunito Sans
Welcome home.
Rounded and friendly without being childish. Nunito Sans is one of the warmest sans-serifs available. Its gentle curves pair beautifully with Fraunces' organic shapes. Ideal for a community-first brand.

04. Guidelines
Usage & Tone
The brand should always feel warm, welcoming, and rooted in community. Every touchpoint, from the website to printed materials, should invite people in and feel genuine.

Voice Principles

Try to speak with warmth over polish. Plain language tends to work better than religious jargon. Aim to be inviting without being pushy, and consider letting silence and space do the work. Not every surface needs to shout.

Photography

Lean toward authentic, warmly-lit photography with natural earth tones. Candid moments often feel better than posed shots. A slight desaturation with warm tones can help. Try to show people who feel real, relatable, and diverse.

Iconography

Thin-stroke, rounded icons tend to pair well with the cross mark's weight. A 1.5px stroke width at standard sizes is a good starting point. Icons should feel gentle and minimal rather than heavy or illustrative.

Digital Applications

Consider using the display scale for website headers on Sand or Warm White backgrounds. Clay buttons with Warm White text work well, with the body font at 13px medium weight and generous padding. Rounded corners around 8 to 12px tend to fit the feel.

Do
  • Give the logo generous whitespace
  • Pair Clay accents with neutral backgrounds
  • Save the display serif for headlines
  • Keep a warm, muted tone across all imagery
  • Let the design breathe. Less is more
Avoid
  • Placing the logo on busy or vibrant backgrounds
  • Using more than two typeface weights per layout
  • Adding drop shadows, gradients, or gloss to the logo
  • Using cold blues or neon colors outside the palette
  • Stretching, rotating, or altering the logo proportions
05. Photography
Real Moments
Our photography should feel like a window into our community, not a curated performance. We want images that are warm, candid, and grounded — capturing people as they really are, in moments that feel genuine and alive.
Reference Original
Brand Warm Earth Treatment
☀

Warm Light

Lean into natural, golden-hour tones. Warmly lit scenes create intimacy and welcome. Avoid harsh flash or cold fluorescent feels. If shooting indoors, edit toward warmth.

◉

Candid & Real

Capture people in authentic moments — laughing, serving, praying, connecting. Posed group shots have their place, but candid images carry more emotional weight for the brand.

◐

Earth Tones

A slight desaturation paired with warm color grading keeps imagery cohesive with the brand palette. Muted greens, soft clay tones, and warm shadows feel right.

Suggested Edit Recipe

These CSS-equivalent adjustments can be replicated in Lightroom, VSCO, or any editing tool. The goal is gentle warmth and softness — not a heavy filter.

Saturation −22% Sepia +8% Brightness 0% Contrast +10% Hue Shift −2°
Show people who feel real, relatable, and diverse.
Photography Guideline
Do
  • Capture candid, in-the-moment interactions
  • Use natural or warm artificial lighting
  • Include a diversity of ages, backgrounds, and settings
  • Apply a consistent warm, muted color grade
  • Show real community life — small groups, worship, service
Avoid
  • Over-saturated or heavily filtered imagery
  • Cold or clinical color tones
  • Stock photography that feels staged or generic
  • Harsh direct flash or unflattering lighting
  • Images that exclude or misrepresent the community
06. Identity
Who We Are
The beliefs and values that shape everything we do. These pillars guide our mission, our community, and our faith.
We make disciples and multiply by building a community that brings glory to God.
We are committed to the following core values that shape how we live, serve, and grow together.
Saviour
The cross typifies Jesus Christ our Saviour. He died on a cross for our sins, and only through Him can we be made right with God.
John 3:16
Sanctifier
The laver illustrates Christ as our Sanctifier. The laver, a large basin used in God's temple for washing, represents cleansing from sin by the power of the Holy Spirit.
1 Thessalonians 5:23-24
Healer
The pitcher symbolizes Christ our Healer. It speaks of oil for divine life and physical healing.
Isaiah 53:4-5
Coming King
The crown stands for Christ our Coming King. He will come back to this earth and reign forever.
Acts 1:11
We are committed to:
01 The Glory of the Triune God +
We love Him wholeheartedly, worship Him sincerely and seek passionately to complete the work He has given us to do.
02 The Authority of the Bible +
We adhere to our statement of faith as a summary of key truths from the Bible that we agree on as a family of churches. We affirm the ministries of preaching and teaching in order to understand, obey, and apply the Bible to our faith and practice.
03 Christ-centred Living +
We lead believers into the fullness of the Spirit and faith in the all-sufficient Christ for healing and power to live holy lives of service.
04 The Church +
We love the Church universal and seek to plant and nurture healthy, transformational local churches as the means of building Christ's Kingdom and nurturing individual Christians. We minister on the basis of spiritual giftedness as essential to divine power and effectiveness. We encourage dynamic creativity resulting in diverse congregational composition, language, and organizational structures in order to fulfill Christ's desire to reach all people.
05 Lost People +
We allocate a disproportionately large amount of energy and resources to reaching lost people, discipling them to maturity in, and devotion to, Christ.
06 Missions +
We give priority to world evangelization and to reaching people who have had the least opportunity to hear the Gospel.
07 Leadership +
We intentionally identify and train godly leaders. We affirm and empower our pastors, other credentialed workers, and lay leaders, esteeming them highly in the Lord.
08 Strategic Cooperation +
We serve each other with humility and compassion. We create strategies, structures, and policies that enhance local initiative and maximize cooperation in the accomplishment of our purpose. We develop strategic partnerships with those of like-minded message, mission, passion, and purpose.
09 Social Responsibility +
We uphold biblical justice. We minister to the poor and oppressed as ministry to Jesus Himself. We encourage and strengthen marriages and families.
10 Stewardship +
We view all our resources and possessions as God-owned and we use them with integrity, accountability, and maximum effectiveness. We embrace faithfulness and sacrifice in the advance of Christ's Kingdom.
11 Prayer +
We do nothing until we pray.
01 God +
There is one God, who is infinitely perfect, existing eternally in three persons: Father, Son and Holy Spirit.
Isaiah 44:6; 45:5-6 | Matthew 5:48; Deut. 32:4 | Matthew 3:16-17; 28:19
02 Jesus +
Jesus Christ is true God and true man. He was conceived by the Holy Spirit and born of the Virgin Mary. He died upon the cross, the Just for the unjust, as a substitutionary sacrifice, and all who believe in Him are justified on the ground of His shed blood. He arose from the dead according to the Scriptures. He is now at the right hand of the Majesty on high as our great High Priest. He will come again to establish His kingdom of righteousness and peace.
Philippians 2:6-11 | Hebrews 2:14-18 | Matthew 1:18 | Luke 1:35 | 1 Corinthians 15:3-5 | Hebrews 4:14-15; 9:24-28 | Matthew 25:31-34; Acts 1:11
03 Holy Spirit +
The Holy Spirit is a divine Person, sent to indwell, guide, teach and empower the believer, and to convince the world of sin, of righteousness and of judgment.
John 14:16-17 | John 16:7-11 | 1 Corinthians 2:10-12
04 Bible +
The Old and New Testaments, inerrant as originally given, were verbally inspired by God and are a complete revelation of His will for the salvation of people. They constitute the divine and only rule of Christian faith and practice.
2 Timothy 3:16 | 2 Peter 1:20-21
05 Sin +
Humankind, originally created in the image and likeness of God, fell through disobedience, incurring thereby both physical and spiritual death. All people are born with a sinful nature, are separated from the life of God, and can be saved only through the atoning work of the Lord Jesus Christ. The destiny of the impenitent and unbelieving is existence forever in conscious torment, but that of the believer is everlasting joy and bliss.
Genesis 1:27 | Romans 8:8 | 1 John 2:2 | Matthew 25:41-46 | 2 Thessalonians 1:7-10
06 Freedom from Sin +
Salvation has been provided only through Jesus Christ. Those who repent and believe in Him are united with Christ through the Holy Spirit and are thereby regenerated (born again), justified, sanctified and granted the gift of eternal life as adopted children of God.
Titus 3:5-7 | Acts 2:38 | John 1:12 | 1 Corinthians 6:11
07 Christian Living +
It is the will of God that in union with Christ each believer should be sanctified thoroughly, thereby being separated from sin and the world and fully dedicated to God, receiving power for holy living and sacrificial and effective service toward the completion of Christ's commission. This is accomplished through being filled with the Holy Spirit which is both a distinct event and progressive experience in the life of the believer.
1 Thessalonians 5:23 | Acts 1:8 | Romans 12:1-2 | Galatians 5:16-25
08 Healing +
Provision is made in the redemptive work of the Lord Jesus Christ for the healing of the mortal body. Prayer for the sick and anointing with oil as taught in the Scriptures are privileges for the Church in this present age.
Matthew 8:16-17 | James 5:13-16
09 Church +
The universal Church, of which Christ is the Head, consists of all those who believe on the Lord Jesus Christ, are redeemed through His blood, regenerated by the Holy Spirit, and commissioned by Christ to go into all the world as a witness, preaching the Gospel to all nations. The local church, the visible expression of the universal Church, is a body of believers in Christ who are joined together to worship God, to observe the ordinances of Baptism and the Lord's Supper, to pray, to be edified through the Word of God, to fellowship, and to testify in word and deed to the good news of salvation both locally and globally. The local church enters into relationships with other like-minded churches for accountability, encouragement and mission.
Ephesians 3:6-12; 1:22-23 | Acts 2:41-47 | Hebrews 10:25 | Matthew 28:19-20
10 Life After Death +
There shall be a bodily resurrection of the just and of the unjust; for the former, a resurrection unto life; for the latter, a resurrection unto judgment.
1 Corinthians 15:20-23 | 2 Thessalonians 1:7-10
11 Second Coming of Christ +
The second coming of the Lord Jesus Christ is imminent and will be personal and visible. As the believer's blessed hope, this vital truth is an incentive for holy living and sacrificial service toward the completion of Christ's commission.
1 Thessalonians 4:13-17 | 1 Corinthians 1:7 | Titus 2:11-14 | Matthew 24:14; 28:18-20
Risen Church
